subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Special Needs Preparedness
description: Learn Special Needs Preparedness in one minute.

hook.text: A diabetic without insulin. An infant without formula. A wheelchair user and stairs. Plan for them now.

buildup.text: Standard disaster plans assume able-bodied adults. Many households include vulnerable members.

discovery.text: Medical devices, medications, mobility aids, and dietary needs require specific planning.

twist.text: Pets are special needs too—30% of Katrina holdouts refused to evacuate without their animals.

climax.text: Registering with local emergency services ensures priority assistance for special needs households.

punchline.text: A plan that doesn't include everyone isn't a plan. It's a gamble.

quiz.question: Why did 30% of Hurricane Katrina holdouts refuse to evacuate?
options: They wouldn't leave without their pets
